activitiesRaw.name: Strength
activitiesRaw.description:
PULL STRENGTH (Heavy)
Lat Pulldown – 5x5
Seated Row – 4x8
Single Arm DB Row – 3x10 each
Face Pull – 3x15
Barbell or DB Curl – 3x10–12
Core: Cable woodchoppers – 3x12 each side

activitiesRaw.name: Strength
activitiesRaw.description:
PUSH STRENGTH (Heavy)
DB Bench – 5x5 (heavy)
Seated Shoulder Press – 4x6–8
Weighted/Assisted Dips – 4x8
Cable Chest Fly – 3x12
Triceps Pushdown – 3x12
Core: Hanging knee raises – 3x15

activitiesRaw.name: HIIT
activitiesRaw.description:
Conditioning (Gym Version)
cycling  5 minutes 
Option B: Rower (if ankle tolerates)
Goal HR: 140–160 bpm.
treadmill - 5 minutes 
You should sweat properly.

activitiesRaw.name: Inclined Treadmill Walk and Stair Stepper
activitiesRaw.description:
30 min inclined treadmill walk at 6%

15 minute stairs stepper at 3 pace

activitiesRaw.name: Upper body pull
activitiesRaw.description:
Upper Body Pull
1️⃣ Lat Pulldown – 4x10
2️⃣ Seated Row Machine – 4x12
3️⃣ Face Pull – 3x15
4️⃣ Dumbbell Biceps Curl – 3x12
5️⃣ Pushups – 3x15
Core: Hanging knee raises 3x12

activitiesRaw.name: Back to the gym after 3.5 months
Terrace Walking
activitiesRaw.description:
Zone 2 (Progression)
45 minutes roof walking.
Add slight incline if possible (stairs carefully).
If HR too low → slightly faster pace.

activitiesRaw.name: Walk and Climb Stairs with Boot on
activitiesRaw.description:
Zone 2 (Roof Session)
Duration: 40 minutes
Heart Rate Target: 120–135 bpm
Focus:
Heel strike
Roll through midfoot
Push off big toe
No rushing
Last 5 minutes barefoot if safe.
This builds Everest engine.

activitiesRaw.name: Reminded of why I started
activitiesRaw.description:
Upper Body Strength (Push Focus)
Warmup:
5 min easy roof walk (boot or stable shoe)
Scapular pushups x15
Arm circles
Main Work:
1️⃣ Pushups (slow 3 sec down) – 4x12
2️⃣ Parallette dips – 4x10
3️⃣ Pike pushups – 3x12
4️⃣ Band chest press – 3x15
Core finisher:
Plank 45 sec
Side plank 30 sec each side
Dead bug x20
3 rounds
Intensity: RPE 7
You should feel worked.

activitiesRaw.name: Light Exercise and A Long walk with the boot on and one crutch
Start of the comeback
activitiesRaw.description:
Went to the doctor yesterday and got a green light to start putting weight on the leg and start walking. 

Wednesday, February 4th

Mobility Drills:
Ankle mobility: Alphabetize ankle circles (3 sets of 10 reps)
Toe spreads and curls: 3 sets of 15 reps
Core:
Plank (using Parallettes for support): hold for 30-60 seconds, RPE 9/10
Hangboard:
Hangboard deadhangs: 2 sets of 20-second hold, RPE 7/10

activitiesRaw.name: PULL + GRIP + SWEAT
activitiesRaw.description:
Good session - Felt really good and strong, although the sleep has been impacted today, slept well for only 4.5 hours. We continue the progress.

Tomorrow and the day after will be travel days so expect a break.


SWEAT BLOCK (Density Intervals)
30 sec ON / 30 sec OFF × 18–24 rounds
Rotate:
Band punches
Seated band curls
Band triceps press
Supported hangboard hold
STRENGTH BLOCK — PULL + GRIP
Seated band rows
4 × 20
Hangboard holds (FEET SUPPORTED, JUGS ONLY)
6 × 10 sec
Band face pulls
4 × 15
Scapular pulls (supported)
3 × 10

activitiesRaw.name: Core + Sweat
activitiesRaw.description:
Its been a good start to the week, will do as much as I can because I will be missing workouts on Wednesday and Thursday due to Travel.

MONDAY — CORE + SWEAT
SWEAT BLOCK (25–30 min)
5–6 continuous rounds
Seated band rows × 25
Seated band chest press × 20
Parallette shoulder shrugs × 15
Band punches × 40 total
Nasal breathing × 60 sec
STRENGTH BLOCK — CORE + PARALLETTES
Parallette support hold
5 × 25–35 sec
Modified L-sit compression (good leg only)
4 × 20–30 sec
Side plank (knees)
3 × 45 sec / side
Parallette shoulder shrugs
3 × 15

activitiesRaw.name: PULL + GRIP + SWEAT
activitiesRaw.description:
Didn't workout on Saturday, but balanced it by working out today which was supposed to be rest day.

Felt okay this week, on and off, mentally okay, wouldn't say amazing, but okay.


SWEAT BLOCK (Density Intervals)
30 sec ON / 30 sec OFF × 18–24 rounds
Rotate:
Band punches
Seated band curls
Band triceps press
Supported hangboard hold
STRENGTH BLOCK — PULL + GRIP
Seated band rows
4 × 20
Hangboard holds (FEET SUPPORTED, JUGS ONLY)
6 × 10 sec
Band face pulls
4 × 15
Scapular pulls (supported)
3 × 10

activitiesRaw.name: Core + Sweat
activitiesRaw.description:
Another day, Another session, its important to knock these off one by one to get to a good state.

I felt groggy in the morning as I was thinking of a idea all night and couldn't sleep well. But glad to tick this session off 


SWEAT BLOCK (25–30 min)
5–6 continuous rounds
Seated band rows × 25
Seated band chest press × 20
Parallette shoulder shrugs × 15
Band punches × 40 total
Nasal breathing × 60 sec

activitiesRaw.name: Thursday Recovery
activitiesRaw.description:
I am doing the best I can with what I have, but obviously I'm not sweating as much as I would like to or am satisfied with the work. But need to be patient and recover first

SWEAT BLOCK (45–60 min, easy pace)
Cycle continuously:
Seated band rows
Band presses
Light core
Breathing
This replaces long hikes mentally.

activitiesRaw.name: Getting back V3
activitiesRaw.description:
It was a light session, considering that I did not do much over the last 3 days, this was a good restart.

SWEAT BLOCK — CORE SWEAT (40 min continuous)
Rotate with minimal rest:
Dead bug × 10 / side
Hollow hold × 30 sec
Side plank × 30 sec / side
Pallof press × 15 / side
STRENGTH BLOCK — PUSH + CHEST
Parallette push-ups (knees)
4 × 12–15
Seated band chest press
4 × 15–20
Pike lean hold on parallettes
4 × 30–45 sec
Triceps press-downs
3 × 20

activitiesRaw.name: Upper Body Metcon
activitiesRaw.description: After a couple days of rest (forced due to low energy and mood swings), i am back with a high volume upper body workout. Shoulders and chest are on fire, was a good workout.

activitiesRaw.name: Core Strength
activitiesRaw.description: A lazy session which focused on core. I feel I should be challenged more, but due to my inability to use my leg there are some restrictions. Will come up with more ways to focus on core.
